body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-free Node.js JSON REST API where users can sign up, sign in, and manage their own private notes, with all data surviving a server restart.

## Spec
ESM Node.js server built only on the standard library — no external web framework and no runtime npm dependencies. Use `node:http` for the HTTP layer and a persistent on-disk store: prefer the built-in `node:sqlite` (a database file on disk); if unavailable in the runtime, fall back to an equivalent on-disk store (e.g. an atomically-written JSON file). In-memory-only storage is not acceptable — users and notes MUST survive a process restart; the store's directory is created on startup if missing.

Server binds to `process.env.PORT` (default 3000). All request and response bodies are JSON (`Content-Type: application/json`).

Endpoints:
- POST /signup {username, password} → 201 {id, username}. Username MUST be unique; duplicate → 409. Passwords are never stored in plaintext (hash + per-user salt) and never returned.
- POST /login {username, password} → 200 {session}, where `session` is an opaque, unguessable token (crypto-random). Wrong credentials → 401.
- GET /me → 200 {id, username} for the bearer-authenticated user.
- POST /notes {title, body} → 201 with the created note (including its id and owner association).
- GET /notes → 200 JSON array of ONLY the authenticated user's notes.

Auth: protected endpoints (/me, POST /notes, GET /notes) require `Authorization: Bearer <session>`; missing/invalid/expired token → 401. A user can only ever read/write their own notes (no cross-user access).

Error handling: malformed JSON, missing/blank required fields, and wrong content shapes → 400; unknown routes/methods → 404/405. Errors return a JSON body; the server never crashes on bad input.

## Changes
- `src/server.js` — creates
- `src/store.js` — creates
- `src/auth.js` — creates
- `test/api.test.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing
- `.gitignore` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: POST /signup with {username, password} creates a user and responds 201 with JSON {id, username}; the password is not present in the response.
- [ ] AC-2: POST /signup with a username that already exists responds 409 and does not create a duplicate.
- [ ] AC-3: POST /login with correct credentials responds 200 with JSON {session}; login with wrong credentials responds 401.
- [ ] AC-4: GET /me with a valid `Authorization: Bearer <session>` responds 200 with {id, username} matching the signed-in user; with a missing or invalid token it responds 401.
- [ ] AC-5: POST /notes with {title, body} and a valid bearer token responds 201 with the created note (id + fields), associated with the authenticated user.
- [ ] AC-6: GET /notes with a valid bearer token responds 200 with a JSON array containing only the authenticated user's notes and none belonging to other users.
- [ ] AC-7: Data persists across a restart: users created and notes added before stopping the server are still retrievable via /login, /me, and /notes after the process is restarted against the same on-disk store.
- [ ] AC-8: The server listens on process.env.PORT and defaults to 3000 when PORT is unset.
- [ ] AC-9: Malformed JSON or missing required fields on POST /signup, /login, and /notes respond 400 with a JSON error body and the server stays running.
- [ ] AC-10: package.json defines an npm "start" script that launches the server.
- [ ] AC-11: The implementation adds no external runtime dependencies (dependencies stay empty; server uses only Node built-in modules).

## Verify
- npm test (unit)
- npm run typecheck (validate)
- npm run lint (validate)

## Non-Goals
- No external web framework or runtime npm dependencies
- No web/HTML UI — JSON API only
- No user roles, note sharing, note update/delete, or pagination
- No email verification or password reset flows

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Deliver a minimal, self-contained multi-user notes service with durable on-disk persistence and bearer-token auth using only the Node standard library."} -->

title: JSON REST API for user accounts and personal notes with on-disk persistence
